preflight.note: Created before implementation work starts for ForgeLink operator cockpit and native product experience.

acceptance_criteria.text: Define decision-first navigation around Decisions, People, Agents, and Channels so action-required decisions are not buried inside ordinary conversations.
Add triage lanes for needs decision, waiting on agent, informational, failed/needs repair, muted, expired, and completed states.
Add relationship-aware grouping for operator, family, trusted humans, external contacts, agents, systems, unknown, and blocked entities.
Add operator availability modes such as available, focus, driving, sleeping, family, work, emergency-only, and offline that affect routing/redaction/batching/escalation.
Add privacy-preserving local presence signals such as app focus, idle, battery, network, DND, paired mobile proximity, and approved calendar integration where applicable.
Add emergency/crisis rules including emergency contact bypass, repeated urgent escalation, agent emergency impersonation prevention, safety-sensitive handling, and local-only fail-safe behavior.
Define mobile companion MVP UX as a paired human decision terminal with redacted alerts, approval cards, approve/deny, short reply, presence signal, emergency toggle, and device revoke.
Add mobile decision terminal flow design for signed approve/deny/defer/request-more-info/short-reply decisions returning to desktop without full private database replication.
Add batch approvals with approve all, approve selected, deny all, and inspect individual items while preserving individual audit/outcome records.
Add human fatigue budget tracking interruptions, urgent interruptions, denials, expirations, response time, and repeated interruptions by agent to recommend batching/deferring/muting.
Add agent reputation UI using approvals, denials, expired requests, malformed requests, failed outcomes, modified-scope attempts, and repeated urgent requests without silently granting authority.
Add local semantic thread summaries for what happened, open decisions, pending replies, last human action, and agent-relevant constraints as derived artifacts.
Add scoped MCP resources such as pending approvals, contact summary, thread summary, and agent status while preventing raw communication dumps by default.
Add reviewed outbox for agent-drafted external messages with review, edit, approve, deny, schedule, and send behavior.
Add channel redaction previews so operators can see what each channel will reveal before dispatch.
Build a reproducible synthetic killer demo showing a ForgeWire/Codex release approval, evidence pack, mobile redacted alert, operator approval, agent publish, outcome record, and replay.
Add public-facing screenshots and narrative positioning ForgeLink as human-boundary infrastructure rather than a Twilio app, using synthetic/redacted data.
Add optional first-run sample workspace with fake contacts, fake agents, fake approvals, fake outcomes, and fake channel states that cannot be confused with real data.

Make local semantic summaries (OCX-012) injection-resistant: treat thread and agent content as untrusted, use injection-resistant prompting, show clear provenance, keep summaries advisory only, and never let summarized content elevate authority or trigger actions.

Define the distribution and update strategy for the expanded surface: code-signed desktop releases with an auto-update channel and a signed build/update path for the mobile companion, as a prerequisite for shipping the mobile companion (OCX-007/008) and the public demo; coordinate with item 011 PR-014.
